summary: The main claim is: The goals of DeepMind, OpenAI, and all the other top research labs are fundamentally opposed to the goal of alignment.

While I admit there is at least a chance this might work, and it IS worth doing technical alignment research, the indications we have so far from the most respected people in the field are that this is an extremely hard problem and there i…
The main claim is: I am currently researching how large-language models reason in normal-form games.
OpenAI have a github repo - evals - for benchmarks for their models.

The main claim is: That may or may not be the best example, but I'm sure LW is quite familiar with the concept that lies at the heart of the problem of AI alignment: what humans consider the morally superior solution isn't always the most "rational" answer.
The problem I foresee is that the different instances of AGI would be as diverse in their ethical systems as humans are.
The main claim is: If my online ads were 100% replaced by DALL-E art, I would probably click on at least 50% more of them.
Below, I've compiled a list of observations made about DALL-E, along with examples.
The main claim is: Inflection AI (website, announcement) is a new startup founded by Mustafa Suleyman, Reid Hoffman, and Karén Simonyan.
The main claim is: A new trend of large-scale models split off the main trend in late 2015.
The main claim is: I think this paper is probably the most exciting machine learning paper I've read so far this year and I'd highly recommend others check it out as well.
Edit: Maybe not; I think Paul's skeptical take here is quite reasonable.
The main claim is: So, human values are fragile, vague and possibly not even a well defined concept, yet figuring it out seems essential for an aligned AI.
For someone not working in the area of ML or AI alignment, it seems obvious that researching simpler-than-human values might be a way to make progress.
